meta_title: For Students: SaaS starter kit to Solve no version control (Intermediate Idea List)
meta_description: Learn how to SaaS starter kit and overcome no version control. A Intermediate-level Idea List using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Students: SaaS starter kit to Solve no version control
content.seo_description: Learn how to build a SaaS starter kit and overcome no version control. An Intermediate-level guide using Antigravity and Claude Code for your portfolio.
content.answer_block: Eliminate the chaos of manual file management by implementing a robust SaaS starter kit with integrated Git version control. By automating your repo initialization and environment configuration, you ensure every iteration of your student project is tracked, secure, and ready for deployment without the risk of data loss.
content.intro:
You’re staring at a folder of 'project_final_v2_real' and sweating. You’ve just spent six hours coding a feature, only to overwrite your entire database config by accident. The absence of version control isn't just a technical oversight; it’s the graveyard where most promising student projects go to die.

But the industry doesn't work in silos, and neither should you. By architecting a professional SaaS starter kit, you transform from a coder who writes scripts into an engineer who builds systems. This is your transition from 'hobbyist' to 'professional,' creating a foundation that protects your hard work and scales alongside your ambition.

steps.title: Initialize Versioned Repository
steps.description: Establish a professional Git foundation to track every change. This step prevents the 'file-copying' nightmare by creating a structured repository.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a professional .gitignore and README.md structure for a Next.js SaaS starter kit that excludes sensitive API keys and node_modules.
steps.claude_code_command: git init && git add . && git commit -m 'Initial commit: project structure'

steps.title: Automate Environment Configuration
steps.description: Abstract environment variables to prevent hardcoding secrets and ensure your project runs consistently across development and production environments.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a template environment file (.env.example) for a SaaS application including placeholders for database URLs, Stripe keys, and Auth providers.

steps.title: Implement Modular Branching
steps.description: Adopt a Git flow strategy to isolate new features. This ensures your main branch remains stable while you experiment with experimental AI features.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Explain a standard Git branching workflow for a solo developer building a SaaS, focusing on feature-branch development.

steps.title: Automate Dependency Auditing
steps.description: Integrate automated checks to ensure your project dependencies are secure and up-to-date, minimizing technical debt.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a script to automate dependency updates and run a vulnerability scan for a Node.js project.
steps.claude_code_command: npm audit fix && git commit -am 'Update dependencies and audit security'

steps.title: Continuous Integration Pipeline
steps.description: Configure a basic CI pipeline to validate your code on every push, ensuring you never break the build.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Provide a GitHub Actions workflow YAML that runs linting and unit tests on every pull request to the main branch.

pro_tips: Always commit in atomic chunks; one logical change per commit makes debugging significantly easier.
Use conventional commits (e.g., 'feat:', 'fix:', 'docs:') to generate automatic changelogs for your portfolio.
Never commit directly to main; treat your main branch as the production-ready source of truth.
Use Git hooks to run linting automatically before a commit is finalized, ensuring code quality standards.
Tag your releases using semantic versioning to track your project's evolution as you build your portfolio.
content.next_steps: Once your repository is stable, deploy your starter kit to Vercel or Railway to see your project live. Use the URL as the centerpiece of your technical portfolio.
content.conversion_block: You now have the framework to build professional-grade software that tracks its own history. Don't let your code exist in a vacuum—take control of your workflow and start building your legacy today. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
